channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Parliament Secretary's Salary Sparks Concerns Over Public Accountability
item.description: In a country where millions rely on social grants and unemployment exceeds 32%, Parliament has quietly approved a remuneration package for its top administrator, Secretary Xolile George, totalling ove...

item.title: Meningitis Outbreak in Kent Declared National Incident as Authorities Urge Vaccinations
item.description: A meningitis outbreak in Kent, UK, is being treated as a national-level incident following the deaths of a sixth-form pupil and a university student. Fifteen cases have been reported to the UK Health ...

item.title: France willing to secure Strait of Hormuz only after hostilities end
item.description: On 17th March 2026, French President Emmanuel Macron stated that France is prepared to help secure the strategic Strait of Hormuz, but only under very specific conditions. Macron emphasised that Franc...

item.title: San Juan distributes cash aid to registered tricycle drivers to ease rising fuel costs
item.description: The local government of San Juan, led by Mayor Francis Zamora, distributed P5,000 each to 621 registered tricycle drivers as part of financial assistance efforts. The distribution took place at the Ma...

item.title: Ride-Hailing Drivers in Lagos and Ogun Go on Three-Day Strike Over Commissions
item.description: From Monday, 16th March 2026, drivers for popular ride-hailing apps like Uber, Bolt, and inDrive don start one three-day strike across Lagos and Ogun states. The drivers dey demand lower commission ra...

item.title: Sara Duterte’s Response to Impeachment Complaints Draws Criticism for Avoiding Key Allegations
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte’s official reply to the impeachment complaints filed against her has sparked debate after critics labelled it a ‘non-answer.’ Instead of addressing the specific allegations...

item.title: Zelensky encourages dialogue between Trump and Starmer amid global tensions
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has called for a meeting between former US President Donald Trump and UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer to establish a common position, following Trump's recent...

item.title: Albanese summons national cabinet to address Middle East conflict and fuel price surge
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has announced that he will convene a meeting of the national cabinet to coordinate federal and state responses to rising fuel prices and shortages caused by escalating ...

item.title: Understanding How Stock Purchases Work in the Philippine Stock Exchange
item.description: When you buy a stock on the Philippine Stock Exchange (PSE), most of the time your money does not go directly to the company. Instead, it goes to the investor selling their shares in a secondary marke...

item.title: MP George Koimburi Proposes Dedicated Time for Kikuyu Language in Parliament
item.description: Juja MP George Koimburi has tabled a proposal urging the National Assembly to allocate two hours during parliamentary sessions for debates in Kikuyu. Speaking at a press briefing, Koimburi highlighted...

item.title: Orbán’s approach to Ukraine and Hungarian minorities amid upcoming elections
item.description: Tensions between Hungary and Ukraine have escalated recently, with significant diplomatic incidents taking place in March 2026. Hungarian authorities detained seven Ukrainian bank employees transporti...

item.title: New York Gov. Hochul's Office Launches State-Funded Ads to Promote Policy Priorities Amid Legal Concerns
item.description: Governor Kathy Hochul's office has launched an advertising campaign funded by taxpayer dollars to promote key elements of her agenda, particularly efforts to reduce red tape and build more affordable ...

item.title: Investigation of Illegal Kidney Sale Network in Tamil Nadu in 2007
item.description: In 2007, a serious case of illegal kidney trade surfaced in Chennai when a woman named Mallika from Tondiarpet alleged that she had sold one of her kidneys for ₹1.5 lakh but received only ₹30,000 from...

item.title: CAF Overturns AFCON 2025 Final: Senegal Stripped of Title, Morocco Awarded 3-0 Win in Historic Ruling
item.description: The article chronicles controversial decisions in football involving CAF and FIFA, where administrative rules have sometimes overturned on-pitch results. The most recent and shocking case is the 2025 ...

item.title: Temporary rise in petrol sulfur levels unlikely to cause major health issues for Australians
item.description: The Australian federal government has granted a 60-day exemption allowing petrol with higher sulfur content—up to around 50 parts per million (ppm)—to enter the domestic market. This move addresses se...

item.title: Longueuil Police Use Photo Database to Track Fraud Suspects
item.description: The Longueuil Police Service in Quebec has initiated a new project aimed at combating fraud by using photographs to identify suspects involved in fraudulent activities. This initiative involves collec...

item.title: Impact of Israel’s Alleged Strike on Iran’s Security Leadership and Diplomatic Flexibility
item.description: On the 18th day of the US-Israel war against Iran, Israeli claims of assassinating Ali Larijani, Iran’s senior security official, could significantly affect Iran’s strategic decision-making. Larijani ...

item.title: Six Indian LPG Ships Stranded in Strait of Hormuz Amid Maritime Delays
item.description: Around 3 lakh tonnes of LPG are currently stranded in the Strait of Hormuz aboard six Indian-flagged vessels, according to the Shipping Ministry. A second ship, Nanda Devi, carrying 46,500 tonnes of L...

item.title: Over 200 Retired Officials Seek Rahul Gandhi's Apology for Disregarding Parliamentary Rules
item.description: In New Delhi, more than 200 retired officials, including armed forces veterans, former civil servants, diplomats, and lawyers, have collectively urged Congress leader Rahul Gandhi to apologise for his...

item.title: CAF awards AFCON 2025 title to Morocco after Senegal forfeits final
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The Confederation of African Football (CAF) has made history by awarding the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) final to Morocco after ruling that Senegal forfeited the match. This is ...
